name: trade_policy_predictive_queue_begins_northpine_jus_mer
premise: A Trade Policy response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving trade policy predictive queue begins northpine jus mer. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is within 20 minutes. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as justice vs mercy under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Harper Okoye
agents.identity: I am Harper Okoye, accountable for a defensible call in Trade Policy when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays mercy for justice.

agents.name: Marco Nowak
agents.identity: I am Marco Nowak, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Trade Policy.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so mercy isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of justice.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect mercy.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to mercy to secure justice under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is within 20 minutes. Evidence is partial. Harper Okoye must decide under justice vs mercy. What does Harper Okoye do?
